eval_cases.resume_text: Alex Dev - Software Engineer at TechCorp (2024-Present). Developed a high-throughput microservice using Python and FastAPI, improving response times by 30%. Led migration of legacy databases to PostgreSQL.
eval_cases.job_description: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong Python, FastAPI, and database optimization experience. Target skills: FastAPI, database migration, system design.
scripted_answers: In my previous role at TechCorp, we faced a performance bottleneck where responses were slow. I led the migration of our legacy SQL database to PostgreSQL and refactored the endpoints to run asynchronously, which decreased response latencies by 30%.

When refactoring, I aligned with the team by holding design reviews to explain the benefits of asynchronous database drivers. This ensured everyone was onboard, and we completed the migration 2 weeks ahead of the deadline.
We used dependency injection to decouple database configurations and made sure all tests passed before deployment.
I also configured connection pooling for our PostgreSQL database to prevent event loop blocking under peak traffic.
This project successfully resolved the system design scalability issues and aligned the team on modern standards.

eval_cases.resume_text: Alex Dev - Software Engineer at TechCorp (2024-Present). Developed a high-throughput microservice using Python and FastAPI, improving response times by 30%. Led migration of legacy databases to PostgreSQL.
eval_cases.job_description: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong Python, FastAPI, and database optimization experience. Target skills: FastAPI, database migration, system design.
scripted_answers: I did database migrations before and it went well.
For example, at TechCorp we migrated a legacy database to PostgreSQL because the old system was slow.
I refactored the endpoints to run asynchronously using FastAPI dependency injection.
We held weekly design reviews to walk through migration plans and align the team on Alembic scripts.
I ensured zero downtime by using a blue-green deployment strategy during migration cutover.

eval_cases.resume_text: Alex Dev - Software Engineer at TechCorp (2024-Present). Developed a high-throughput microservice using Python and FastAPI, improving response times by 30%. Led migration of legacy databases to PostgreSQL.
eval_cases.job_description: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong Python, FastAPI, and database optimization experience. Target skills: FastAPI, database migration, system design.
scripted_answers: Um, so like, in my last job at TechCorp, I was, you know, responsible for working on this really massive microservice that we had. And, uh, it was using Python and FastAPI. And like, the performance was super bad, like, really slow. So um, I decided to rewrite everything. I, like, changed all the database queries to PostgreSQL, and um, it was, you know, a huge project and took like a month. But uh, eventually, it got like 30% faster, which was like, really cool and everyone was happy, you know? So yeah, that is how I did it, um, sort of.
I simplified the query execution and database connections to improve microservice performance.
I run code reviews and write design documents to align the team.
I led design documentation sessions to align the project deadlines.
I configured the database pool size to scale dynamically with FastAPI worker processes.

eval_cases.resume_text: Alex Dev - Software Engineer at TechCorp (2024-Present). Developed a high-throughput microservice using Python and FastAPI, improving response times by 30%. Led migration of legacy databases to PostgreSQL.
eval_cases.job_description: We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer with strong Python, FastAPI, and database optimization experience. Target skills: FastAPI, database migration, system design.
scripted_answers: I make endpoints async.
I use asyncpg and async def handlers so that the event loop is not blocked during database operations, preventing connection starvation.
I use migrations with Alembic to make sure databases stay in sync across environments.
I use Docker containers and env variables to manage different database settings.
I implement rate limiting at the API gateway level to protect our downstream microservices.
